artifact.title: Work Task Lifecycle
artifact.trigger: A project manager creates a new work task in draft state for a project.

transitions.guard: Due date must fall within the project start and end dates
Task must be assigned to a worker before it can be started

story.goal: Create work tasks, assign them to field workers with due dates, and track their progress through to completion.
steps: The project manager opens the project detail view and creates individual work tasks with clear descriptions of what needs to be done.
The project manager assigns each task to a specific field worker and sets a due date that falls within the project start and end dates.
The field worker starts working on the assigned task and updates its status to in progress so the PM and dashboard reflect current progress.
The field worker completes the work and marks the task as completed, recording hours and materials along the way.
The project manager reviews the simplified task timeline and delay risk suggestions to ensure the project stays on track.
story.outcome: All work tasks are created, assigned to field workers, and progress through to completion with full timeline and budget visibility for the project.
